name: 3 Sum
category: Solve Problems on Arrays [Easy -> Medium -> Hard]
statement:
Given an integer array nums. Return all triplets such that:


  • i != j, i != k, and j != k


  • nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0.


Notice that the solution set must not contain duplicate triplets. One element can be a part of multiple triplets. The output and the triplets can be returned in any order.

examples.explanation: nums[1] + nums[2] + nums[0] = 0 nums[4] + nums[1] + nums[5] = 0 nums[4] + nums[2] + nums[3] = 0
nums[1] + nums[2] + nums[0] = 0 Note that we have used two -1s as they are separate elements with different indexes But we have not used the -1 at index 4 as that would create a duplicate triplet

nowYourTurn.input: nums = [8, -6, 5, 4] (Give answer with the output and triplets sorted in ascending order)
